PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Continued To Apr-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Cole for and with (petitioner) Joan King.

The Court stated that we are here on a petition for letters of administration with a holographic will and asked counsel if there was any additional support for allowing the holographic will. Mr. Cole responded that they are still attempting and may be able to get proof from the decedent's stock broker, that they have the decedent's check book and passport with signatures as well as other documents signed by her and her doctor which they will file.
Ms. King confirmed that the doctor is still around and that they may be able to get confirmation from him but that the decedent had not been seen by the doctor since 2012. The Court stated that it is inclined to continue the matter to obtain additional proof, that it is not inclined to allow a blocked account in lieu of bond and that it is unclear as to what the relationship between the decedent and Ms. King actually is. Ms. King clarified that the decedent was the sister of her Uncle. The Court stated that it is not an issue yet but that at some time it will likely require evidence to overcome presumption of undue influence. Mr. Cole stated that the decedent took care of her own affairs, that the blood relatives have been noticed and requested a short continuance date as the bank has sent a letter regarding escheating the funds in the account to the state. The petition for letters of administration with will annexed was continued to April 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
